short_name: Improving 24-hour Blood Pressure Stability in Spinal Cord Injury With Low Oxygen Therapy
title: Improving 24-hour Blood Pressure Stability in Spinal Cord Injury With Low Oxygen Therapy

skipped_criteria: Living with a chronic cervical (at or above T1) spinal cord injury of at least one year: no schema field captures injury chronicity, specific spinal level, or duration of injury.
Are fluent in English: no schema field captures language fluency.
High-cervical injuries requiring ventilator assistance to breathe will be excluded: no schema field captures ventilator dependency or specific injury level.
Unhealed fracture, contracture, or pressure sore that might interfere with protocol completion: no schema field captures wound or musculoskeletal status.
Additional concurrent neurological conditions (e.g., multiple sclerosis, Parkinson disease, stroke or brain injury): no schema field captures comorbid neurological diagnoses.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular or pulmonary disease: no schema field captures cardiovascular or pulmonary disease status.
Severe neuropathic pain: no schema field captures pain status.
Severe recurrent autonomic dysreflexia: no schema field captures autonomic dysreflexia history.
History of seizure disorders: no schema field captures seizure history.
